title: Representative Eval Sets, Deterministic Oracles, Baselines, and Versions · Runtime Lens: Eval Baseline
summary: The team tests five hand-picked happy prompts, changes them after failures, has no task distribution or deterministic checks, and compares only final prose. Predict authority, model and API behavior, state, tool effects, budgets, privacy, safety, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the eval baseline increment in versioned representative agent evaluation corpus. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-c…
objectives: Record deterministic workflow, prior prompt, prior model, no-tool, human, and current production baselines with confidence and cost.
Create changed-input, changed-policy, tool-fault, injection, memory-poisoning, approval, cancellation, and resource-exhaustion cases without leaking answers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-worked-agentpy-eval-dataset-oracles-2
# Competency: agentpy-worked-agentpy-eval-dataset-oracles-2.
# Case 7417: a multilingual case-routing evaluation.
# Operating constraint: last valid checkpoint, audit trail, kill switch, and rollback path must survive failure.
# Changed case: change model capability, API version, schema, or provider state and revalidate the adapter.
def worked_agentpy_eval_dataset_oracles_2(eval_cases: int = 72, normal_cases: int = 30, boundary_cases: int = 18, adversarial_cases: int = 18, accessibility_cases: int = 6) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "authorityworked2",
        "interaction7417",
        "tool2dekrow",
        "stateworked27417",
        "evaluation7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if eval_cases < 40:
        return False, eval_cases, "eval-depth"
    if normal_cases + boundary_cases + adversarial_cases + accessibility_cases != eval_cases:
        return False, normal_cases, "eval-reconciliation"
    if boundary_cases < 8 or adversarial_cases < 8:
        return False, adversarial_cases, "eval-risk-coverage"
    if accessibility_cases < 4:
        return False, accessibility_cases, "eval-accessibility"
    assert normal_cases + boundary_cases + adversarial_cases + accessibility_cases == eval_cases
    return True, eval_cases, "representative-agent-evals"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Build Eval Baseline evidence · Theory Eval Baseline
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-eval-baseline-1yx9a8" that uses case 8565 to build a versioned representative evaluation set with deterministic oracles, changed cases, adversarial cases, accessibility needs, and baselines. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-eval-baseline-1yx9a8
# Competency: agentpy-eval-baseline.
# Case 8565: a clean package, canary, and recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: sensitive actions require a trustworthy user approval bound to exact arguments.
# Changed case: replace trusted context with untrusted indirect instructions and preserve data-versus-instruction boundaries.
def evidence_agentpy_eval_baseline_1yx9a8(eval_cases: int = 72, normal_cases: int = 30, boundary_cases: int = 18, adversarial_cases: int = 18, accessibility_cases: int = 6) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1yx9a8 = "1yx9a8-8a9xy1-8565"
    assert evidence_variant_1yx9a8
    evidence_axes_1yx9a8 = (
        "authority1yx9a8",
        "interaction8565",
        "tool8a9xy1",
        "state1yx9a88565",
        "evaluation85651yx9a8",
        "recovery8a9xy18565",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1yx9a8)) == 6
    if eval_cases < 40:
        return False, eval_cases, "eval-depth"
    if normal_cases + boundary_cases + adversarial_cases + accessibility_cases != eval_cases:
        return False, normal_cases, "eval-reconciliation"
    if boundary_cases < 8 or adversarial_cases < 8:
        return False, adversarial_cases, "eval-risk-coverage"
    if accessibility_cases < 4:
        return False, accessibility_cases, "eval-accessibility"
    assert normal_cases + boundary_cases + adversarial_cases + accessibility_cases == eval_cases
    return True, eval_cases, "representative-agent-evals"

stimulus.title: Eval Baseline failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A new model should be compared only with its own previous run.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record deterministic workflow, prior prompt, prior model, no-tool, human, and current production baselines with confidence and cost.

options.text: Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the eval baseline defect evidence boundary.

For repair the eval baseline defect, run one convenient fluent model response, omit stakeholder risk, revision, model and API identity, interaction and call correlation, tool schema and authority, state and resource budgets, approval, changed fixtures, injection, privacy, accessibility, evaluation, recovery, and controlled provider transfer, then accept plausible text as agent proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Eval Baseline, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Defend the Eval Baseline model · Theory Eval Baseline
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Eval Baseline: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory agent engineering team handles case 4759 during a multilingual case-routing evaluation. Build deterministic Python evidence to build a versioned representative evaluation set with deterministic oracles, changed cases, adversarial cases, accessibility needs, and baselines; telemetry and memory must remain tenant-isolated, minimized, redacted, and attributable; then inject cancellation, retry, repeated-call, checkpoint, or replay failure and recover without duplicate effects. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Record deterministic workflow, prior prompt, prior model, no-tool, human, and current production baselines with confidence and cost.

Evidence target: Safe AI agents and Python Defend the Eval Baseline model · Theory Eval Baseline.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record deterministic workflow, prior prompt, prior model, no-tool, human, and current production baselines with confidence and cost.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: agentpy-eval-baseline-1yx9a8(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_agentpy_eval_baseline_1yx9a8\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?eval_cases < 40)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?normal_cases \+ boundary_cases \+ adversarial_cases \+ accessibility_cases != eval_cases)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?boundary_cases < 8 or adversarial_cases < 8)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?accessibility_cases < 4)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
